Defines a test command class that prints a message when called.

# Purpose
The code defines a class `TestCommand` that inherits from `BaseCommand`, providing a specific command functionality within a command-line interface framework. The `TestCommand` class sets the `command` attribute to the string `'test'`, indicating its purpose as a test command. The [`__call__`](<#TestCommand__call__>) method is overridden to first invoke the parent class's [`__call__`](<#TestCommand__call__>) method with any arguments passed to it, and then it prints the message "Hi there!" to the console. This code provides narrow functionality, primarily serving as a simple test command implementation within a larger command framework.

- `__future__.print_function`
- `pebble_tool.commands.base.BaseCommand`
